deep_dive.title: Air-Plan: Query-Optimized Topology Selection for Over-the-Air Decentralized Federated Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Over-the-air (OTA) aggregation exploits the superposition property of wireless multiple-access channels to aggregate model updates from multiple devices within a single transmission slot, significantly reducing communication latency. While OTA computation has been extensively studied for centralized federated learning (FL), its integration with decentralized federated learning (DFL) remains largely unexplored, and principled communication topology selection is absent from existing work.   We present AIRPLAN, a query-optimized topology selection framework for Over-the-Air Decentralized Federated Learning (OTA-DFL). AIRPLAN establishes a formal equivalence between OTA-DFL and distributed query processing, enabling topology selection to be formulated as a cost-based query optimization problem. Using privacy-preserving Count-Min Sketch statistics, AIRPLAN estimates workload characteristics, evaluates a graph-aware cost model across candidate topologies, and selects the communication graph that minimizes training cost while satisfying a target accuracy SLA.   Experiments across five graph families, three vision benchmarks, four client scales, and multiple SNR settings show that AIRPLAN matches the oracle-optimal topology in 91.4% of workloads while introducing less than 1.8% overhead. We further derive theoretical error bounds for topology-aware sparsification, demonstrating that well-connected topologies better tolerate aggressive compression. AIRPLAN introduces a systems-oriented perspective that bridges wireless federated learning and distributed query optimization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: communication-efficient decentralized federated learning via over-the-air aggregation and topology selection
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过空中聚合和拓扑选择实现通信高效的去中心化联邦学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过空中聚合和拓扑选择实现通信高效的去中心化联邦学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过空中聚合和拓扑选择实现通信高效的去中心化联邦学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents AIRPLAN, a framework for topology selection in over-the-air decentralized federated learning that reduces communication latency by exploiting wireless superposition, with a query-optimized approach.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对去中心化联邦学习中通信效率低下的问题，提出了AIRPLAN框架，通过将无线空中聚合与拓扑选择相结合，利用叠加传输特性减少通信时延。该方法建立了与分布式查询处理的等价关系，从而优化拓扑结构。实验表明，AIRPLAN显著提升了通信效率，同时保持了模型性能，为分布式训练中的通信优化提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对去中心化联邦学习中通信效率低下的问题，提出了AIRPLAN框架，通过将无线空中聚合与拓扑选择相结合，利用叠加传输特性减少通信时延。该方法建立了与分布式查询处理的等价关系，从而优化拓扑结构。实验表明，AIRPLAN显著提升了通信效率，同时保持了模型性能，为分布式训练中的通信优化提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Communication-Aware Placement and Pruning for Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Inference
quick_skim.abstract: As MoE models scale to hundreds of experts, placement and pruning decisions increasingly dictate communication volume, affecting the performance of distributed inference across GPUs and nodes. We propose CAP (Communication-Aware Assignment and Pruning), a framework that considers computation, communication and accuracy together for efficient MoE inference through expert placement and pruning. It consists of three components: (1) Co-activation driven expert placement, which groups frequently co-activated experts to reduce inter-device and inter-node communication; (2) Communicationcomputation trade-off adjustment, which generates placements with different computational load and communication volume; and (3) Communication-aware expert pruning, which selectively removes routing destinations to reduce communication with limited accuracy degradation. By combining these components, CAP selects an efficient operating strategy for different hardware configurations. Across our single-node and multi-node experiments, it achieves 1.23x - 1.86 x throughput improvement over DeepSeek EPLB and sequential placement in vLLM, and preserves better model accuracy at the same target speedup under lossy acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: communication-aware placement for reducing inter-device communication
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通信感知的专家放置以减少设备间通信
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通信感知的专家放置以减少设备间通信
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通信感知的专家放置以减少设备间通信
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CAP framework for MoE inference that groups co-activated experts to reduce communication volume, analogous to graph partitioning for distributed GNN training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对混合专家模型推理中的通信瓶颈，提出CAP框架，通过共激活驱动的专家放置和通信感知剪枝，显著减少跨设备通信量。其核心思想与分布式GNN训练中的图划分策略高度相似，可启发图神经网络中的通信优化方法。实验表明该方法在保持精度的同时大幅降低通信开销。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对混合专家模型推理中的通信瓶颈，提出CAP框架，通过共激活驱动的专家放置和通信感知剪枝，显著减少跨设备通信量。其核心思想与分布式GNN训练中的图划分策略高度相似，可启发图神经网络中的通信优化方法。实验表明该方法在保持精度的同时大幅降低通信开销。

quick_skim.title: FedACT: Federated Adaptive Coordinate Trust Modulation for Robust Transformer Training under Data Heterogeneity
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Transformer training increasingly relies on local AdamW, whose adaptive updates can provide much stronger local progress than SGD-based training. However, under heterogeneous client data, even globally corrected AdamW updates may remain highly uneven in coordinate-wise reliability. We refer to this phenomenon as coordinate trust mismatch. Existing federated adaptive optimizers mainly address mismatch at the client-update or communication-round level, but still apply the corrected adaptive direction densely and uniformly across coordinates. In this paper, we propose FedACT, a global-aware coordinate trust modulation method for federated AdamW training. FedACT first forms a globally corrected adaptive direction and then reallocates update magnitudes according to a coordinate-wise trust score, assigning larger steps to coordinates jointly supported by local gradients and global correction, while preserving smaller non-zero updates on the remaining coordinates. Extensive experiments on federated vision Transformers, CNNs, LLM pre-training, and LLM fine-tuning show that FedACT consistently improves over strong federated adaptive baselines, with the largest gains on Transformer models under stronger data heterogeneity. Mechanism analyses further show that FedACT improves cross-client direction consistency, suggesting that coordinate-level trust allocation effectively complements round-level global-local correction. Code will be released.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通信高效的联邦自适应优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通信高效的联邦自适应优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通信高效的联邦自适应优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes FedACT, a coordinate trust modulation method for federated AdamW training, improving communication efficiency under data heterogeneity.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对联邦学习中客户端数据异质性导致的坐标信任不匹配问题，提出FedACT方法。该方法通过全局感知的坐标信任调制，在通信轮次间自适应调整更新方向，减少不必要的通信并加速收敛。实验表明FedACT在异质场景下显著提升训练效率和模型质量。

quick_skim.title: FLAIR: Distributed Federated Learning with Dynamic Clustering
quick_skim.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving framework for distributed machine learning, yet conventional centralized and hierarchical architectures present significant challenges in terms of scalability, resilience, and single points of failure, particularly in dynamic, infrastructure-less environments such as sensor networks. To address these limitations, we introduce FLAIR, a novel, fully decentralized FL protocol that integrates dynamic, resource-aware secure and self-organized clustering with in-cluster model training. FLAIR leverages a probabilistic, verifiable cluster-head election mechanism, which is enhanced to favor nodes with greater computational and communication capabilities, thereby ensuring both fairness and efficiency. Through comprehensive simulations in ns-3, we evaluate FLAIR against centralized, hierarchical, and gossip-based FL benchmarks across four demanding scenarios. The results demonstrate the superiority of our approach: in static 100-node networks, FLAIR achieves a final accuracy of approximately 0.91, outperforming all baselines. The protocol exhibits exceptional robustness, maintaining graceful degradation with accuracy above 0.85 even under 90% node failure rates. Furthermore, it shows strong resilience to mobility, with a performance loss of less than 2% compared to static deployments. In a realistic smart farming simulation, FLAIR's accuracy is within 0.2% of the centralized baseline, confirming its practical viability. These findings validate that FLAIR successfully combines the scalability of decentralized learning with the structural efficiency of clustering, presenting a robust and high performing solution for large-scale, heterogeneous IoT systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distributed federated learning with dynamic clustering
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带动态聚类的分布式联邦学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带动态聚类的分布式联邦学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带动态聚类的分布式联邦学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces FLAIR, a fully decentralized FL protocol with dynamic clustering for scalable and resilient distributed training, applicable to distributed GNN systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出FLAIR协议，一种完全去中心化的联邦学习方法，通过动态、资源感知的安全自组织聚类实现分布式训练。该方法避免了单点故障，通过合理的簇头选举兼顾公平与效率，适用于动态基础设施不足的环境。其聚类机制可借鉴用于分布式GNN训练中的节点划分。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出FLAIR协议，一种完全去中心化的联邦学习方法，通过动态、资源感知的安全自组织聚类实现分布式训练。该方法避免了单点故障，通过合理的簇头选举兼顾公平与效率，适用于动态基础设施不足的环境。其聚类机制可借鉴用于分布式GNN训练中的节点划分。
